suggestions.title: workers.json missing `id` field -- breaks character identity across the entire app
suggestions.description: Workers fetched from workers.json have no `id` field. Cascading failures: (1) activeIds becomes Set([undefined]), so ALL DEFAULT_ROSTER members pass the ghost filter -- Maya and Sofia appear TWICE on the office floor, once working and once as a ghost. Visible in the live screenshot. (2) fadingMap, seenIdsRef, newIds, and focusedWorker all key on w.id, so new-arrival animations, fade-outs, and click-to-focus are broken. (3) React key={w.id} on WorkerCard renders all cards with key undefined, causing silent DOM reconciliation errors. (4) CharacterAvatar never renders the named custom SVG avatars for active workers -- they all fall through to the emoji/generic fallback. Fix: add an id field to each worker in workers.json matching the lowercase first name (e.g. id: maya).

suggestions.title: workers.json uses `startedAt` but code expects `updated_at` -- silently kills duration, ETA, stuck-worker UI
suggestions.description: Every time-based feature reads worker.updated_at, but workers.json stores the field as startedAt. Broken features: working-duration badge (never renders), ETA in progress bar (always null), stuck-worker detection in StatsBar (never triggers), idle duration badge in the office (blank), ghost desk last-seen tooltip (blank). Fix: rename the field in workers.json from startedAt to updated_at, or add a mapping in syncFromGitHub. Also, App.jsx line 408 references stuckWorkers[0].started (a third variant of the field name that does not exist) -- that should be updated_at.

suggestions.title: workers.json missing `progress` field -- all progress bars stuck at 0%, rings never appear
suggestions.description: Workers in workers.json have no `progress` field. ProgressBar receives undefined, coercing to 0 -- bars show empty with a 0% label. The progress ring in the office view never appears (guarded by typeof worker.progress === 'number'). Fix: add a progress integer (0-100) to each worker in workers.json. Also consider adding a roster key so the worker-filter dropdown in the activity log is populated rather than empty.

suggestions.title: Duplicate characters on office floor -- same worker appears as both active and ghost simultaneously
suggestions.description: Because workers.json workers lack an id field, activeIds in Office.jsx becomes Set([undefined]). The ghost filter !activeIds.has(w.id) passes for every DEFAULT_ROSTER member (since 'maya' and 'sofia' are not undefined), so Maya and Sofia are rendered at their working desks AND as ghost characters at additional desks. This is directly visible in the live screenshot. Root cause is the missing id bug above, but the symptom deserves its own callout as the most user-visible regression.

suggestions.title: Activity and worker filter bars use role=radio on <button> elements with broken keyboard navigation
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 1007-1036 use <button role='radio'> for activity and worker filters. Buttons with role=radio should be navigable with arrow keys within the group, not Tab. Currently Tab moves between each button individually (tab-stop flood) with no arrow-key navigation inside the group. The outer role=radiogroup wrapper is correct but the keyboard contract is not fulfilled. Fix options: (a) use real <input type='radio'> styled as buttons, or (b) keep <button> elements, remove role=radio, and use aria-pressed to indicate selection.

suggestions.title: Vibe streak resets on every page reload -- sessionStorage defeats the streak concept
suggestions.description: The vibeStreak counter reads from sessionStorage, which is cleared when the browser tab is closed or the page is reloaded. A streak disappearing on reload feels punishing and undermines the keep-the-vibe-going mechanic. Switching to localStorage would persist streaks across sessions. Since the streak is purely cosmetic, stale data risk is negligible. Note also that the streak badge only appears at vibeStreak >= 3, so frequent-reloader users never see it.

suggestions.title: All office characters unconditionally get tabIndex=0 including non-interactive ghost workers
suggestions.description: The Character component in Office.jsx always renders with tabIndex=0. Ghost characters (OOO roster members) show a hover bubble on focus but have no click handler and no keyboard action. Screen-reader and keyboard users must tab through every ghost before reaching other interactive elements. Fix: set tabIndex=0 only when the character has an onClick handler, and tabIndex=-1 (or omit) for ghost characters. Also remove role=button from non-clickable characters.

suggestions.title: Keyboard shortcuts (1, 2, R, Esc) are completely undiscovered -- no hint visible in the UI
suggestions.description: The app implements useful keyboard shortcuts but they are only hinted in title attributes of tab buttons (desktop hover only). They are invisible on mobile and unknown to screen-reader users. Add a small ? icon button in the header that opens a keyboard shortcuts popover, or add a persistent kbd-styled hint strip below the tab bar. This rewards power users and makes the app feel more intentional.

suggestions.title: Mobile office view is functional but barely usable -- 24px avatars, hidden names, no touch optimization
suggestions.description: At 375px viewport the office floor collapses to 280px min-height with 24x24px avatars and character names hidden (font-size: 0 at <480px). Tap targets are well below the 44x44px accessibility minimum. The char-hover-card tooltip appears on hover, which does not exist on touch. Suggested fixes: (a) enlarge the office floor with horizontal scroll/pan on mobile, (b) replace hover-card with a tap-to-show overlay, or (c) default to the dashboard tab on narrow screens where the office is impractical.

suggestions.title: No visual feedback on the Office tab during sync -- Bloberto typing dots are too subtle
suggestions.description: When the sync button is pressed or auto-poll fires, the only feedback on the Office tab is a small typing-dots indicator above Bloberto. A user pressing the sync button (R or click) on the Office tab has no obvious confirmation that data is updating. The Dashboard tab clearly shows sync status via the StatsBar. Add a subtle pulsing border or dim overlay on the office floor during isSyncing, or show a small syncing chip near the tab bar.

suggestions.title: Polling silently stops after 10 minutes of inactivity -- live dot stays green but data is stale
suggestions.description: The IDLE_TIMEOUT of 10 minutes stops polling without any visual feedback. A user who leaves the tab open sees stale data with no indication; the live dot stays green if the last fetch was successful. Add a 'Paused -- move mouse to resume' chip, or change the live dot to yellow/amber when polling is suspended, to prevent misleading status.

suggestions.title: Favicon canvas re-created on every poll cycle -- should only update when indicator color actually changes
suggestions.description: The favicon useEffect in App.jsx re-runs whenever activeWorkers or fetchError changes (every 30s poll), each time creating a new Canvas, drawing a circle, converting to a data URL, and replacing the link element. The favicon can only be one of three colors (red/green/grey). Derive a single status string ('error' | 'working' | 'idle') and use that as the effect dependency instead, preventing canvas allocation on every poll.

suggestions.title: No React Error Boundary -- a single component crash silently takes down the entire app
suggestions.description: There is no Error Boundary anywhere in the component tree. If any component throws during render (e.g. malformed worker data, unexpected null from the API), the entire React tree unmounts and users see a blank page with no explanation. Add an ErrorBoundary wrapping at least the Office component and WorkerCard grid, showing a graceful fallback with a retry button. Given the app consumes static JSON with no schema validation, a bad field value will silently break the whole UI.

suggestions.title: completionToast uses 12 inline style declarations that cannot be themed or motion-reduced
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 822-843 style the completion toast entirely with inline style={{...}}, including position, colors, border, border-radius, padding, font-size, z-index, animation, and box-shadow. The hardcoded rgba box-shadow does not adapt to light theme. The animation cannot be overridden by prefers-reduced-motion. Extract to a .completion-toast CSS class to fix theming, motion, and maintainability in one move.

suggestions.title: No API schema validation -- silent failures when data fields are missing or renamed
suggestions.description: The app fetches two JSON files and consumes them without any validation. Missing fields (as demonstrated by the id/updated_at/progress bugs) fail silently. At minimum, add console.warn in development when expected fields like id, updated_at, or progress are absent. For a more robust solution, a lightweight schema check at the top of syncFromGitHub would surface data contract mismatches immediately during development.

suggestions.title: WORKER_ROLE_COLORS in App.jsx duplicates ROLE_COLORS data, keyed by worker name instead of role
suggestions.description: App.jsx defines WORKER_ROLE_COLORS mapping worker names to colors (lines 14-20). The same color values already exist in ROLE_COLORS in constants.js mapped by role. WORKER_ROLE_COLORS is used only for confetti color and would break for any new worker whose name is not listed. Replace with ROLE_COLORS[worker.role] to derive confetti color from the worker role -- no new mapping needed.

suggestions.title: getRelativeTime logic duplicated: named function in App.jsx and identical inline IIFE in Office.jsx
suggestions.description: The relative-time calculation (mins < 1 -> 'just now', mins < 60 -> Xm ago, etc.) is implemented twice: as getRelativeTime in App.jsx (line 34) and as an identical inline IIFE inside the ghost OOO bubble render in Office.jsx (lines 432-441). Move both to utils/time.js and import the shared function. This also makes the formatting logic independently testable.

suggestions.title: Footer version string v1.0.0-chaos is hardcoded and will drift from package.json
suggestions.description: The footer renders a hardcoded v1.0.0-chaos string. In a Vite project this is trivially fixed: import { version } from '../package.json' (Vite supports JSON imports by default) and render the value as v${version}-chaos. This keeps the displayed version in sync with the actual package version automatically.

suggestions.title: No notification when a worker enters error state for background users -- only favicon changes
suggestions.description: When a worker hits error status, the UI reacts visually (red card border, glitch animation, error badge on Dashboard tab), but a user with the app in a background tab only notices the favicon color change. Consider a subtle Notification API call (with user permission) or an aria-live='assertive' announcement when a new error worker appears, making this useful for on-call monitoring scenarios where keeping the tab visible is not practical.

suggestions.title: Office floor plants are tiny emoji at fixed percentage positions -- invisible on mobile
suggestions.description: The decorative plants are emoji characters at fixed percentage coordinates, rendering at roughly 16-20px on mobile. They add charm on desktop but become invisible blobs at small sizes. Consider replacing with small inline SVGs (consistent with the desk lamp and away-sign approach) so they scale with the floor, or use CSS font-size: clamp(0.6rem, 1.5vw, 1.2rem) so they remain legible at all viewport widths.

suggestions.title: Activity log entry count label reflects unfiltered total when a worker or type filter is active
suggestions.description: In App.jsx line 997, the label reads 'Last {Math.min(activityLog.length, 20)} events'. This counts the unfiltered log, not the filtered result. If a worker filter is active and that worker has no log entries, the label might say 'Last 15 events' while the visible log is empty. Change the count to reflect the post-filter entry count, and add a 'No entries for this filter' empty state for clarity.
